content.problemStatement:
Chef recently created a new Tiling Window Manager called ChefWM. He wants to find out the maximum number of columns of windows he can fit on the screen and he needs your help.

Consider the screen to be an axis-aligned rectangle in the xyxyxy-plane, with its bottom-left corner at (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0) and top-right corner at (N,M)(N, M)(N,M) (that is, the screen has width NNN units and height MMM units). ChefWM works as follows:


First, the screen is divided into **one or more** columns of **equal integer width**.
Then, each column is divided into **two or more** windows of **equal integer height**. Note that all windows within a column must have the same height, but windows in different columns may have different heights.
Finally, Chef would like the following condition to hold:

No two windows from different columns can have their horizontal boundary share the same yyy-coordinate (apart from y=0y = 0y=0 and y=My = My=M). See the figures below for more details.


It is not allowed to leave any empty space on the screen, i.e, the division performed in the first two steps above must cover the entire screen.

Here is an example of a tiling that satisfies all the conditions, for N=8N = 8N=8 and M=6M = 6M=6.

Below are two tilings that satisfy all conditions except the last. The one on the left has a horizontal edge at y=2y = 2y=2 in both columns, while the one on the right has a horizontal edge at y=2y = 2y=2 in column 111 and column 333.

What is the **maximum** number of columns that ChefWM can fit on the screen while following the above conditions? Note that you **do not need to maximize the number of windows**, you only need to maximize the number of columns.

It may be possible that no columns can be created under these conditions, in that case, print 000.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers N,MN, MN,M — the coordinates of the top-right corner of the screen.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing one integer — the **maximum** number of columns ChefWM can create for the screen.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: It is possible to make 444 columns as follows:

The first column has 777 windows of height 303030 each
The second column has 222 windows of height 105105105 each
The third column has 555 windows of height 424242 each
The last column has 333 windows of height 707070 each

It can be seen in the diagram below that the edges from any two windows from different columns do not have the same yyy-coordinate except for y=0y=0y=0 and y=my=my=m.

Test Case 2: There can only be one column no matter how many windows you keep (as long as there are at least 222 windows).
Test Case 3: You can have a single column with 555 windows. It can be shown that this is the maximum number of columns that can possibly satisfy the given restrictions.
